meta.title: Organizing the U.S. Government to Counter Islamist Extremism
meta.description: For all the progress the United States has made in fighting terrorist networks, there has been a general failure to confront the terrorism problem's ideological center of gravity. A new Hudson Institute study examines how the U.S. government could mount an effort to address this failure by working to change the ideological climate in the Muslim world.  The study identifies which types of governmental and nongovernmental organizations should be created to conduct this effort. Produced by Dougla
